5. Look for manifest.json schema - search for "manifest" in the codebase

9. Look for ExecuteAttempt or similar structs that hold attempt state

- Full list of files in cli/cmd/
- Key structs and functions in agent execution (file paths + line numbers)
- Metrics schema (what fields are in attempts.jsonl)
- Manifest.json schema 
- Any existing replay-related code

18	// landingGitOpsFromFactory returns the agent.LandingGitOps the CommandFactory
19	// should use — either the test override or the default RealLandingGitOps.
20	func landingGitOpsFromFactory(f *CommandFactory) agent.LandingGitOps {
21		if f.executeBeadLandingGitOverride != nil {
22			return f.executeBeadLandingGitOverride
23		}
24		return agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
25	}

27	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
28		cmd := &cobra.Command{
29			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
30			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
31			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
32	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
33	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
34	
35	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
36	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
37	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
38	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
39	
40	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
41	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
42	
43	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx work", which claims ready beads,
44	calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on the result.
45	Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a specific bead.
46	
47	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
48	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
49	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
50	  no_evidence_produced             — agent exited with no commit and no rationale
51	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
52	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
53	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
54	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
55	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
56	
57	ddx work closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
58	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
59	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
60			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "ddx work" for normal queue work)
61	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
62	
63	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
64	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
65	
66	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
67	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
68	
69	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
70	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
71			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
72			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
73		}
74		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
75		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
76		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")

91	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
92		beadID := args[0]
93		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
94			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
95		}
96		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
97		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
98		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
99		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
100		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
101		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
102		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
103		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
104		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
105		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
106		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
107		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
108		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
109		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
110	
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
114			Harness:       harness,
115			Model:         model,
116			Provider:      provider,
117			ModelRef:      modelRef,
118			Effort:        effort,
119			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
120			MinPower:      minPower,
121			MaxPower:      maxPower,
122		}
123		if requestTimeout > 0 {
124			overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
125		}
126		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the queue-work preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`

127	func (f *CommandFactory) resolveCommitSHA(commitSHA string) (string, error) {
128		if commitSHA == "" {
129			return "", nil
130		}
131	
132		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
133		defer cancel()
134	
135		repoDir := f.WorkingDir
136		if repoDir == "" {
137			repoDir = "."
138		}
139	
140		if isFullCommitSHA(commitSHA) && !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoDir) {
141			return commitSHA, nil
142		}
143	
144		cmd := gitpkg.Command(ctx, repoDir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commitSHA+"^{commit}")
145		out, err := cmd.Output()
146		if err != nil {
147			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", commitSHA, err)
148		}
149		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil

88	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer appends are safe. Creates parent
89	// directories if needed. Best-effort callers may discard the returned error.
90	func AppendRow(projectRoot string, row AttemptRow) error {
91		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
92		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
100		data, err := json.Marshal(row)
101		if err != nil {
102			return err
103		}
104		data = append(data, '\n')
105		_, err = f.Write(data)
106		return err
107	}

164	// BackfillFromEvents processes beads' event streams, appending one AttemptRow
165	// per attempt found (identified by kind:cost events). Deduplicates by
166	// attempt_id: rows already in attempts.jsonl are skipped. Returns the count of
167	// new rows added.
168	//
169	// The matching algorithm: for each kind:cost event, scan forward to find the
170	// next kind:execute-bead event (which carries the finalization outcome). This
171	// pair gives the attempt's cost/token/timing data and its loop-level outcome.
172	func BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot string, beads []BeadAttemptEvents) (int, error) {
173		seen, err := LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot)
174		if err != nil {
175			return 0, err
176		}
177	
178		var added int
179		for _, b := range beads {
180			rows := extractRowsFromEvents(b)
181			for _, row := range rows {
182				if _, exists := seen[row.AttemptID]; exists {
183					continue
184				}
185				if err := AppendRow(projectRoot, row); err != nil {
186					return added, err
187				}
188				seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
189				added++
190			}
191		}
192		return added, nil
193	}

195	// extractRowsFromEvents derives one AttemptRow per kind:cost event found in
196	// b.Events. Each cost event is paired with the immediately following
197	// kind:execute-bead event to obtain the outcome.
198	func extractRowsFromEvents(b BeadAttemptEvents) []AttemptRow {
199		var rows []AttemptRow
200		for i, ev := range b.Events {
201			if ev.Kind != "cost" {
202				continue
203			}
204			var cost costEventBody
205			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &cost); err != nil || cost.AttemptID == "" {
206				continue
207			}
208	
209			// Scan forward for the next execute-bead event to get outcome.
210			outcome := ""
211			for _, next := range b.Events[i+1:] {
212				if next.Kind == "execute-bead" {
213					outcome = next.Summary
214					break
215				}
216			}
217	
218			tsEnd := ""
219			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
220				tsEnd = ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
221			}
222			// Approximate ts_start from ts_end and duration.
223			tsStart := ""
224			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() && cost.DurationMS > 0 {
225				start := ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Add(-time.Duration(cost.DurationMS) * time.Millisecond)
226				tsStart = start.Format(time.RFC3339)
227			}
228	
229			total := cost.TotalTokens
230			if total == 0 {
231				total = cost.InputTokens + cost.OutputTokens
232			}
233	
234			row := AttemptRow{
235				SchemaVersion: SchemaVersion,
236				AttemptID:     cost.AttemptID,
237				BeadID:        b.BeadID,
238				SpecID:        b.SpecID,
239				TSStart:       tsStart,
240				TSEnd:         tsEnd,
241				Model:         cost.Model,
242				Harness:       cost.Harness,
243				Provider:      cost.Provider,
244				Outcome:       outcome,
245				ExitCode:      cost.ExitCode,
246				CostUSD:       cost.CostUSD,
247				DurationMS:    cost.DurationMS,
248				InputTokens:   cost.InputTokens,
249				OutputTokens:  cost.OutputTokens,
250				TotalTokens:   total,
251			}
252			rows = append(rows, row)
253		}
254		return rows
255	}

150	func Attempt(ctx context.Context, store Store, beadID string, opts AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error) {
151		if opts.Executor == nil {
152			return Outcome{}, fmt.Errorf("try attempt: executor is required")
153		}
154		rateCfg := resolveRateLimitRetryConfig(opts)
155		var (
156			report Report
157			err    error
158		)
159		for retryCount, elapsed := 0, time.Duration(0); ; {
160			report, err = opts.Executor.Execute(ctx, beadID)
161			if err != nil {
162				report = Report{
163					BeadID: beadID,
164					Status: StatusExecutionFailed,
165					Detail: err.Error(),
166					Error:  err.Error(),
167				}
168			}
169			if report.BeadID == "" {
170				report.BeadID = beadID
171			}
172			if report.Status == "" {
173				report.Status = StatusExecutionFailed
174			}
175	
176			rateLimited := IsRateLimitReport(&report)
177			effectiveBudget := rateCfg.Budget
178			if opts.RateLimitBudget == 0 && report.RateLimitBudget != 0 {
179				effectiveBudget = report.RateLimitBudget
180			}
181			decision, retryAfter := evaluateRateLimitAttempt(report, rateCfg, retryCount+1, elapsed, effectiveBudget)
182			if !decision.ShouldRetry {
183				if decision.Reason != "" {
184					report.Error = decision.Reason
185				}
186				if rateLimited && decision.Reason != "" && rateCfg.OnRetry != nil {
187					rateCfg.OnRetry(ctx, RateLimitRetryInfo{
188						Attempt:    retryCount + 1,
189						Source:     decision.Source,
190						Report:     &report,
191						Elapsed:    elapsed,
192						RetryAfter: retryAfter,
193						OverBudget: true,
194					})
195				}
196				break
197			}
198	
199			retryCount++
200			info := RateLimitRetryInfo{
201				Attempt:    retryCount,
202				Wait:       decision.Wait,
203				Source:     decision.Source,
204				Report:     &report,
205				Elapsed:    elapsed,
206				RetryAfter: retryAfter,
207			}
208			if rateCfg.OnRetry != nil {
209				rateCfg.OnRetry(ctx, info)
210			}
211			if err := rateCfg.Sleep(ctx, decision.Wait); err != nil {
212				return Outcome{}, err
213			}
214			elapsed += decision.Wait
215		}
216	
217		if ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery(report, opts.ProjectRoot) {
218			if opts.Store == nil {
219				opts.Store = store
220			}
221			return ConflictRecoveryOutcome(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput{
222				Bead:             opts.Bead,
223				Report:           report,
224				ProjectRoot:      opts.ProjectRoot,
225				AutoRecover:      opts.AutoRecover,
226				ConflictResolver: opts.ConflictResolver,
227				Store:            opts.Store,
228				Assignee:         opts.Assignee,
229				Now:              opts.Now,
230				Cooldown:         opts.Cooldown,
231			}), err
232		}
233	
234		if report.Status == StatusNoChanges {
235			if opts.Store == nil {
236				opts.Store = store
237			}
238			if opts.Store == nil {
239				return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "IncrNoChangesCount", StoreErr: fmt.Errorf("try attempt: no_changes store is required")}, nil
240			}
241			noChangesCount, countErr := opts.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(beadID)
242			if countErr != nil {
243				return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "IncrNoChangesCount", StoreErr: countErr}, nil
244			}
245			noChangesOut, adjudicatedReport, adjErr := adjudicateNoChangesContract(ctx, beadID, report, opts.ProjectRoot, noChangesCount, opts.SatisfactionChecker, opts.VerificationRunner)
246			if adjErr != nil {
247				return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "adjudicateNoChanges", StoreErr: adjErr}, nil
248			}
249			return Outcome{
250				Report:      adjudicatedReport,
251				Disposition: OutcomeReported,
252				NoChanges:   &noChangesOut,
253			}, nil
254		}
255	
256		if report.Status == StatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition {
257			effectiveStore := opts.Store
258			if effectiveStore == nil {
259				effectiveStore = store
260			}
261			if effectiveStore != nil {
262				if err := effectiveStore.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(beadID, bead.StatusOpen, bead.LifecycleTransitionOptions{
263					Reason: "declined: bead requires decomposition before execution",
264					Source: "ddx agent try",
265				}, func(b *bead.Bead) error {
266					if b.Extra == nil {
267						b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
268					}
269					b.Extra[bead.ExtraExecutionElig] = false
270					return nil
271				}); err != nil {
272					return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "UpdateWithLifecycleStatus", StoreErr: err}, nil
273				}
274			}
275			event := buildDecompositionParkingEvent(report)
276			return Outcome{
277				Report:      report,
278				Disposition: OutcomePark,
279				Parking: &ParkingOutcome{
280					Unclaim:              true,
281					RunPostAttemptTriage: true,
282					Event:                event,
283				},
284			}, nil
285		}
286	
287		if report.Status == StatusPushConflict {
288			parkUntil := nowFn(opts.Now).UTC().Add(maxAttemptCooldown)
289			report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
290			event := buildPushConflictParkingEvent(report)
291			return Outcome{
292				Report:      report,
293				Disposition: OutcomePark,
294				Parking: &ParkingOutcome{
295					Unclaim:              true,
296					RetryAfter:           parkUntil,
297					RunPostAttemptTriage: true,
298					Event:                event,
299				},
300			}, nil

121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}

146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}

The `ddx agent replay` command:

- Optionally uses closing commit SHA from `bead.Extra["closing_commit_sha"]` as base
- Supports `--at-head`, `--sandbox`, `--model` overrides
- Runs the agent via `agent.RunWithConfigViaService()`
- Shows token/cost/duration metrics

**Pattern to follow for `ddx bead replay`:**

- Extract attempt metadata from bead.Extra or events
- Build an ExecuteBeadRuntime with the stored manifest
- Call `agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig()` to re-execute

2	// bead-attempt written to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Rows are appended with
3	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer access is safe. The schema_version field

81	// AttemptsPath returns the absolute path to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
82	// under projectRoot.
83	func AttemptsPath(projectRoot string) string {
84		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, attemptsRelPath)
85	}
86	
87	// AppendRow appends row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl under projectRoot using
88	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer appends are safe. Creates parent
89	// directories if needed. Best-effort callers may discard the returned error.
90	func AppendRow(projectRoot string, row AttemptRow) error {
91		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
92		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
100		data, err := json.Marshal(row)
101		if err != nil {
102			return err
103		}
104		data = append(data, '\n')
105		_, err = f.Write(data)
106		return err
107	}

109	// LoadAttemptIDs reads .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl and returns the set of
110	// attempt_ids already present. Used to deduplicate BackfillFromEvents runs.
111	// Returns an empty map when the file does not exist.
112	func LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot string) (map[string]struct{}, error) {
113		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
114		f, err := os.Open(path)
115		if err != nil {
116			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
117				return map[string]struct{}{}, nil
118			}
119			return nil, err
120		}
121		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
122	
123		seen := map[string]struct{}{}
124		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
125		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024) // 1 MiB lines max
126		for sc.Scan() {
127			var row struct {
128				AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id"`
129			}
130			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil || row.AttemptID == "" {
131				continue
132			}
133			seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
134		}
135		return seen, sc.Err()
136	}
137	
138	// LoadRows reads all rows from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Malformed lines
139	// are skipped. Returns an empty slice when the file does not exist.
140	func LoadRows(projectRoot string) ([]AttemptRow, error) {
141		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
142		f, err := os.Open(path)
143		if err != nil {
144			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
145				return []AttemptRow{}, nil
146			}
147			return nil, err
148		}
149		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
150	
151		var rows []AttemptRow
152		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
153		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024)
154		for sc.Scan() {
155			var row AttemptRow
156			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil {
157				continue
158			}
159			rows = append(rows, row)
160		}
161		return rows, sc.Err()
162	}
163	
164	// BackfillFromEvents processes beads' event streams, appending one AttemptRow
165	// per attempt found (identified by kind:cost events). Deduplicates by
166	// attempt_id: rows already in attempts.jsonl are skipped. Returns the count of
167	// new rows added.
168	//
169	// The matching algorithm: for each kind:cost event, scan forward to find the
170	// next kind:execute-bead event (which carries the finalization outcome). This
171	// pair gives the attempt's cost/token/timing data and its loop-level outcome.
172	func BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot string, beads []BeadAttemptEvents) (int, error) {
173		seen, err := LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot)
174		if err != nil {
175			return 0, err
176		}
177	
178		var added int
179		for _, b := range beads {
180			rows := extractRowsFromEvents(b)
181			for _, row := range rows {
182				if _, exists := seen[row.AttemptID]; exists {
183					continue
184				}
185				if err := AppendRow(projectRoot, row); err != nil {
186					return added, err
187				}
188				seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
189				added++
190			}
191		}
192		return added, nil
193	}
194	
195	// extractRowsFromEvents derives one AttemptRow per kind:cost event found in
196	// b.Events. Each cost event is paired with the immediately following
197	// kind:execute-bead event to obtain the outcome.
198	func extractRowsFromEvents(b BeadAttemptEvents) []AttemptRow {
199		var rows []AttemptRow
200		for i, ev := range b.Events {
201			if ev.Kind != "cost" {
202				continue
203			}
204			var cost costEventBody
205			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &cost); err != nil || cost.AttemptID == "" {
206				continue
207			}
208	
209			// Scan forward for the next execute-bead event to get outcome.
210			outcome := ""
211			for _, next := range b.Events[i+1:] {
212				if next.Kind == "execute-bead" {
213					outcome = next.Summary
214					break
215				}
216			}
217	
218			tsEnd := ""
219			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
220				tsEnd = ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
221			}
222			// Approximate ts_start from ts_end and duration.
223			tsStart := ""
224			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() && cost.DurationMS > 0 {
225				start := ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Add(-time.Duration(cost.DurationMS) * time.Millisecond)
226				tsStart = start.Format(time.RFC3339)
227			}
228	
229			total := cost.TotalTokens
230			if total == 0 {
231				total = cost.InputTokens + cost.OutputTokens
232			}
233	
234			row := AttemptRow{
235				SchemaVersion: SchemaVersion,
236				AttemptID:     cost.AttemptID,
237				BeadID:        b.BeadID,
238				SpecID:        b.SpecID,
239				TSStart:       tsStart,
240				TSEnd:         tsEnd,
241				Model:         cost.Model,
242				Harness:       cost.Harness,
243				Provider:      cost.Provider,
244				Outcome:       outcome,
245				ExitCode:      cost.ExitCode,
246				CostUSD:       cost.CostUSD,
247				DurationMS:    cost.DurationMS,
248				InputTokens:   cost.InputTokens,
249				OutputTokens:  cost.OutputTokens,
250				TotalTokens:   total,
251			}
252			rows = append(rows, row)
253		}
254		return rows
255	}
256	
257	// Rfc3339 formats a time.Time as RFC3339 UTC; returns "" for the zero value.
258	func Rfc3339(t time.Time) string {
259		if t.IsZero() {
260			return ""
261		}
262		return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
263	}

48	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
49	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
50	  no_evidence_produced             — agent exited with no commit and no rationale
51	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
52	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
53	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
54	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
55	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
56	
57	ddx work closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
58	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
59	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
60			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "ddx work" for normal queue work)
61	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
62	
63	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
64	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
65	
66	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
67	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
68	
69	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
70	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
71			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
72			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
73		}
74		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
75		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
76		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
77		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
78		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
79		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
80		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
81		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")

91	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
92		beadID := args[0]
93		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
94			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
95		}
96		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
97		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
98		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
99		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
100		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
101		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
102		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
103		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
104		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
105		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
106		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
107		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
108		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
109		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
110	
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
114			Harness:       harness,
115			Model:         model,
116			Provider:      provider,
117			ModelRef:      modelRef,
118			Effort:        effort,
119			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
120			MinPower:      minPower,
121			MaxPower:      maxPower,
122		}
123		if requestTimeout > 0 {
124			overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
125		}
126		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the queue-work preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
157		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
158		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
159		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
160		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
161			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
162				return err
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
167		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
168		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
169	
170		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
171		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
172		if err != nil && res == nil {
173			return err
174		}
175	
176		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
177		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
178		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
179		//
180		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
181		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
182		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
183		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
184		if res != nil {
185			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
186			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
187				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
188			} else {
189				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
190				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
191					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
192				}
193			}
194			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
195				NoMerge:         noMerge,
196				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
197			}
198	
199			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
200			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
201			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
202			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
203			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
204			// duration of gate evaluation.
205			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
206				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
207				if ctxErr != nil {
208					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
209					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
210				} else if wt != "" {
211					defer cleanup()
212					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
213					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
214					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
215					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
216				}
217			}
218	
219			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
220				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
221				_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
222			} else if err == nil {
223				err = landErr
224			}
225		}
226	
227		if err != nil {
228			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
229			return err
230		}
231	
232		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
233	}
234	
235	func writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd *cobra.Command, res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult, asJSON bool) error {
236		if res == nil {
237			return nil
238		}
239		if asJSON {
240			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
241			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
242			return enc.Encode(res)
243		}
244	
245		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "bead:    %s\n", res.BeadID)
246		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "base:    %s\n", res.BaseRev)
247		if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
248			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "result:  %s\n", res.ResultRev)
249		}
250		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "outcome: %s\n", res.Outcome)
251		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "status:  %s\n", res.Status)
252		if res.Detail != "" {
253			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "detail:  %s\n", res.Detail)
254		}
255		if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
256			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "rationale: %s\n", res.NoChangesRationale)
257		}
258		if res.PreserveRef != "" {
259			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "ref:     %s\n", res.PreserveRef)
260		}
261		return nil
262	}

200	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
201	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
202	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
203	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
204	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
205	// worktree.
206	type GitOps interface {
207		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
208		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
209		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
210		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
211		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
212		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
213		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
214		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
215		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
216		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
217		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
218		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
219		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
220		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
221		// other work that might prune it.
222		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
223		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
224		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
225		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
226	}

404	func dirtyWorktreePaths(dir string) []string {
405		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--untracked-files=all").Output()
406		if err != nil {
407			return nil
408		}
409		var paths []string
410		seen := map[string]bool{}
411		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
412			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
413			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || len(line) < 4 {
414				continue
415			}
416			path := strings.TrimSpace(line[3:])
417			if idx := strings.Index(path, " -> "); idx >= 0 {
418				path = strings.TrimSpace(path[idx+4:])
419			}
420			if path == "" || seen[path] {
421				continue
422			}
423			seen[path] = true
424			paths = append(paths, path)
425		}
426		return paths
427	}

12	// LockRetryPolicy describes the retry/backoff curve used when attempting to
13	// acquire a process-shared lock that guards git index operations on the
14	// primary .git (the tracker-commit lock). The policy is owned by this
15	// package per ddx-da11a34a AC#4: callers in cli/internal/triage and
16	// elsewhere consult ActionRetryWithBackoff but do not duplicate the curve
17	// implementation here.
18	//
19	// The curve is exponential with a cap and a bounded max-retries budget so
20	// that lock contention is absorbed silently up to a point, after which it
